questions.question: You have multiple workloads running on your Kubernetes cluster and must collect logs for security and monitoring purposes. To achieve this, you need a log collector running on every node in the cluster. What Kubernetes feature will ensure a log collector pod is always running on every node in the cluster, even as nodes are added and removed from the cluster?
options: ReplicaSet
StatefulSet
DaemonSet
Deployment
correct: DaemonSet
questions.overallExplanation: In Kubernetes, DaemonSets are a type of workload resource that ensures a specific pod runs on every node within a cluster. They're commonly used for tasks that need to be performed on all nodes, such as monitoring agents, log collectors, or network plugins. DaemonSets automatically handles pod scheduling and scaling, ensuring consistent and ubiquitous deployment of these services across the cluster.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/daemonset/

questions.question: What is the name of the collective group of individuals responsible for overseeing the overall direction of the Kubernetes project in the CNCF?
options: Kubernetes Steering Committee
Kubernetes Enhancement Proposals
Special Interest Group
Working Group
correct: Kubernetes Steering Committee
questions.overallExplanation: The Kubernetes Steering Committee is the guiding force for the Kubernetes project within the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF). Their primary function is to provide strategic direction, technical oversight, and governance leadership. They ensure the project remains aligned with the CNCF's objectives, maintain a cohesive technical roadmap, and foster a collaborative community. Additionally, the committee plays a role in coordinating Kubernetes releases, resolving conflicts, representing the project to the broader community, and promoting ecosystem growth through partnerships and integrations. Their efforts contribute to the project's continued development, innovation, and success in the cloud-native landscape.Incorrect Answers:working groups are teams that come together to work on different issuesspecial interest groups are responsible for managing specific areas of the projectKEPs are the standard procedure to collect and document ideas for enhancements to the Kubernetes projecthttps://github.com/kubernetes/steering#steering-committee

questions.question: What security mechanism would you use to secure (encrypt) communication between internal Kubernetes services within a cluster?
options: Transport Layer Security (TLS)
Network Policies
Ingress Controllers
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
correct: Transport Layer Security (TLS)
questions.overallExplanation: To secure communication between internal Kubernetes services within a cluster, you would typically use Transport Layer Security (TLS) to encrypt and secure the communication channels. TLS ensures that data exchanged between services is protected from eavesdropping and tampering.Incorrect Answers:Ingress controllers are responsible for routing external traffic into the cluster, not for securing internal service-to-service communication.Network policies are used to control network traffic between pods based on defined rules but do not provide encryption or secure communication by themselves.RBAC is used for managing access control and permissions within Kubernetes but does not directly secure communication channels between services.https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tls/managing-tls-in-a-cluster/

questions.question: How can you use a selector in Kubernetes to identify and manage resources based on their labels?
options: by creating custom metrics for labeled resources
by configuring ingress controllers for labeled resources
by setting resource quotas to limit the number of labeled resources
by defining labels for resources and using selectors in resource specifications
correct: by defining labels for resources and using selectors in resource specifications
questions.overallExplanation: In Kubernetes, labels are key-value pairs attached to resources, and selectors are used to filter and select resources based on their labels. This mechanism enables efficient grouping, identification, and management of resources within a Kubernetes cluster.Incorrect Answers:resource quotas are used to limit resource consumption (CPU, memory, etc.) and do not directly relate to selecting or managing resources based on labelscustom metrics can be used for monitoring and autoscaling, but they do not pertain to using selectors to identify and manage resources based on labelsIngress controllers are used to manage external access to services in a Kubernetes cluster and do not directly involve using selectors to identify and manage labeled resourceshttps://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/#label-selectors

questions.question: In the event that the Kubernetes control plane becomes unavailable, what happens to the existing pods that are running on a cluster?
options: when the control plane fails, the existing pods will immediately become unavailable
existing pods on the cluster will continue to run without interruption
existing pods will continue to run and support updates or changes as needed
all existing pods on the cluster will immediately be rescheduled on another cluster with a surviving control plane
correct: existing pods on the cluster will continue to run without interruption
questions.overallExplanation: When the Kubernetes control plane becomes unavailable, the existing pods running within the cluster typically continue to run without interruption. This is because the control plane primarily handles cluster management, API server requests, and control logic for desired state reconciliation, but it doesn't directly manage the execution of individual pods once they are scheduled onto nodes.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/components/#control-plane-components

questions.question: After collecting metrics, what popular tool is often integrated with Prometheus to display dashboards and present the metrics in graphical form?
options: Grafana
Kubernetes Dashboard
Elasticsearch and Kibana (ELK Stack)
Docker Compose
correct: Grafana
questions.overallExplanation: After collecting metrics, one popular tool that is often integrated with Prometheus to display dashboards and present the metrics in graphical form is Grafana. Grafana is a widely used open-source platform for monitoring and observability that can be seamlessly integrated with Prometheus to create visually appealing and interactive dashboards. It allows users to build custom dashboards, visualize time-series data, and gain insights into the performance and health of their systems through charts, graphs, and alerts.Incorrect Answers:the Kubernetes Dashboard is a web-based user interface for managing Kubernetes clusters and does not primarily serve as a tool for displaying Prometheus metrics.Docker Compose is a tool for defining and running multi-container Docker applications. It is not typically used for displaying Prometheus metrics.While Elasticsearch and Kibana (ELK Stack) are popular tools for log aggregation and visualization, they are not commonly used for displaying Prometheus metrics. Prometheus metrics are typically visualized using Grafana, which is specifically designed for this purpose.https://grafana.com/

questions.question: What is a container in the context of computing and software development?
options: a type of programming language used to create user interfaces accessed by an organization's customers
a virtual machine used to run multiple applications on a single computer to save on physical resources
a standardized and isolated package used to run applications that include an application and all its dependencies
a physical storage device for files and documents that are consumed by production applications
correct: a standardized and isolated package used to run applications that include an application and all its dependencies
questions.overallExplanation: A container is a lightweight, standalone, and executable package that includes everything needed to run a piece of software, including the code, runtime, libraries, and system tools. Containers provide a consistent and isolated environment for applications, ensuring they run consistently across different computing environments, from development to production. They are widely used in modern software development for their portability, scalability, and efficient resource utilization.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid Kubernetes SIG?
options: architecture
storage
certification
networking
correct: certification
questions.overallExplanation: A Kubernetes SIG, or Special Interest Group, is a self-organized group within the Kubernetes open-source community that focuses on a specific area of the project. Each SIG is responsible for defining and driving forward the development and enhancements related to its designated domain. SIGs facilitate collaboration among individuals and organizations with shared interests, expertise, and goals. They cover a diverse range of topics, including areas like storage, networking, scalability, and user experience. SIGs operate with transparency and follow an open governance model, allowing anyone to participate, contribute, and influence the direction of Kubernetes development in their respective focus area.https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/sig-list.md

questions.question: Which tool would you use to simplify the installation and management of a complex web application on a Kubernetes cluster on Kubernetes?
options: kube-proxy
kubeconfig
kubectl
Helm
correct: Helm
questions.overallExplanation: Helm is a package manager for Kubernetes that simplifies the installation, upgrading, and management of complex applications. It allows you to define, package, and version all the Kubernetes resources required for your application in a structured way using Helm charts. This makes it an ideal choice for deploying multi-component applications like the one described in the scenario.https://helm.sh/

questions.question: What architecture is commonly associated with breaking up a larger, monolith application into smaller, loosely coupled services that represent a specific function or application?
options: microservices
three-tier
serverless
event-driven
correct: microservices
questions.overallExplanation: Microservices is an architectural approach that revolutionizes how software applications are designed and built. It breaks down complex systems into small, independently deployable services, each focused on a specific business capability. These services communicate through APIs, promoting flexibility, scalability, and ease of maintenance. Microservices enable organizations to develop, update, and scale components individually, fostering agility in a rapidly changing technological landscape. While offering benefits such as resilience and technology diversity, they also pose challenges related to inter-service communication and deployment complexity, making careful design and management crucial for success.https://glossary.cncf.io/microservices-architecture/https://glossary.cncf.io/monolithic-apps/

questions.question: In Kubernetes, what is a "Job"?
options: a high-availability configuration for a Kubernetes cluster
it's a container image stored in a Kubernetes repository
a Kubernetes resource used to manage short-lived, batch-processing tasks
a Kubernetes resource used to define a long-running service
correct: a Kubernetes resource used to manage short-lived, batch-processing tasks
questions.overallExplanation: The correct answer is that a "Job" in Kubernetes is a resource used to manage short-lived, batch-processing tasks. Jobs are designed to run to completion, ensuring that a specified number of pods (tasks) successfully complete their work before terminating. They are commonly used for tasks like data processing, backups, or one-time computations.Incorrect Answers:A Kubernetes resource used to define a long-running service describes other resources like Deployments or StatefulSets, not Jobs. Jobs are intended for short-lived tasks with a well-defined endpoint.A container image stored in a Kubernetes repository is simply an image, not a Kubernetes Job. A Job uses a container image to run its tasks, but they are distinct concepts.A high-availability configuration for a Kubernetes cluster does not relate to what a Kubernetes Job is. High-availability configurations involve components like control plane redundancy and node redundancy but are not directly associated with Jobs.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/

questions.question: What is the term for the process of bringing the observed state of an application or system in line with the desired state?
options: reconciliation
autoscaling
observability
microservices
correct: reconciliation
questions.overallExplanation: The term for the process of bringing the observed state of an application or system in line with the desired state is reconciliation. Reconciliation involves automatically detecting and correcting discrepancies between the actual state of a system and the intended or desired state, ensuring that the system remains in a consistent and desired configuration. In cloud-native and DevOps contexts, reconciliation is a fundamental concept for maintaining system health and reliability.https://www.cncf.io/blog/2022/08/10/add-gitops-without-throwing-out-your-ci-tools/

questions.question: You are managing a Kubernetes cluster and need to see a list of all the pods. What command can you run to view all of the pods?
options: kubectl get pods
kubectl pods list
kubectl pods -view-all
kubectl show me the pods
correct: kubectl get pods
questions.overallExplanation: The command used to view the list of pods in a Kubernetes cluster is: kubectl get podsRunning this command will display a list of pods along with their current status, such as Running, Pending, or Terminating, in the specified namespace (or the default namespace if none is specified).Incorrect Answers:the others are all made-up commands - the correct answer is kubectl get podshttps://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/cheatsheet/#viewing-and-finding-resources

questions.question: Which definition offers the most precise representation of logs within the context of system observability?
options: a key/value pair associated with a particular metric generated by a system
the ability to follow a request as it flows through a system or application
records of events that have occurred which contain information about a specific event
a numerical record of data that indicates the state of a particular component or action taken by a user
correct: records of events that have occurred which contain information about a specific event
questions.overallExplanation: Logs are like records of events that happened, and they hold details about those events. There are two main parts of a log: the time it happened (timestamp) and the message that tells us about the event. Often, logs are the most common way a system shows what's happening. Even if you don't have a way to understand everything (observability), you usually have some kind of logs in your program or system. However, using logs might not always be the best way to find and fix problems. Logs can have too much information and be hard to understand. It can be tricky to figure out what went wrong because of all the data. Also, logs might have information from different things happening at the same time, which makes it even harder to review and understand them to solve problems.Incorrect Answers:the ability to follow a request through a system is the definition of a tracea numerical record of data is a metriclogs aren't key/value pairs, although they often contain lots of data about an eventhttps://www.cncf.io/blog/2020/12/04/why-are-you-logging-if-youre-not-using-the-logs/

questions.question: What is the primary function of the Kubernetes project?
options: a platform for scheduling and managing containers at scale
an orchestration platform to quickly build container images
a monitoring solution focused on gathering metrics for containerized applications
an automation platform to run serverless functions on public cloud providers
correct: a platform for scheduling and managing containers at scale
questions.overallExplanation: Kubernetes' primary function is to orchestrate and manage containerized applications within a cluster of servers or nodes. It automates the deployment, scaling, and management of containers, allowing developers to focus on writing code rather than dealing with infrastructure complexities. Kubernetes abstracts away the underlying hardware and provides a consistent and reliable platform for deploying and running applications. It enables features like automatic load balancing, self-healing, and rolling updates, making it a powerful tool for modern application development and ensuring high availability and scalability for containerized workloads.Incorrect Answers:Kubernetes isn't designed to build container images - you can use other tools like docker build or HashiCorp Packer, among othersKubernetes doesn't help you run serverless applications on a public cloud platform. You can consume a hosted Kubernetes platform, like AWS EKS or Azure AKS. You can also use Knative to extend Kubernetes to run serverless functionsKubernetes isn't a monitoring solution - this description is a better fit for Prometheushttps://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the benefits of observability of a system or application?
options: guarantees 100% uptime, even during power outages and hardware failures
ensures that your system is invisible to potential hackers, enhancing security.
the ability to understand and measure the current and/or historical state of a system based on metrics generated by the system or application
gives an organization the ability to transform the software development lifecycle into an entirely automated process
correct: the ability to understand and measure the current and/or historical state of a system based on metrics generated by the system or application
questions.overallExplanation: Observability refers to the ability to understand and measure how a system is doing using the data the system creates. It helps you get useful information from unexpected situations when things are always changing. Observability is important because it helps you know more about how your system or application works inside. It also helps you find and fix problems faster, even if they are difficult to find. You can also watch how well your application is working. Basically, Observability helps you understand your system better. Without it, your application might run, but you won't really know what's happening inside.Observability can help:Provide a deeper understanding of the inner workings of a system or applicationSpeed up troubleshooting efficiencyQuickly identify issues with an application or systemMonitor the performance of a system or applicationObservability is crucial for gaining a comprehensive understanding of the inner workings of your system. Without it, your application will operate blindly, without any insight into its processes.Incorrect Answers:observability doesn't help secure your application or make it invisible to hackersobservability may help with ensuring uptime by using the generated metrics, but it doesn't guarantee uptimeobservability doesn't really impact the software development lifecycle but it could impact it knowing what metrics that you might need to measure forhttps://glossary.cncf.io/observability/

questions.question: Which of the following is a cloud-native architecture that allows developers to run custom server-side code in containers that are commonly managed by a cloud service provider?
options: virtual machines
edge computing
bare metal servers
serverless
correct: serverless
questions.overallExplanation: Serverless computing, often called Function as a Service (FaaS), is a cloud computing model where developers can build and deploy applications without managing the underlying server infrastructure. In a serverless architecture, developers focus solely on writing code for individual functions or services, which are executed in response to events or triggers, such as HTTP requests, database changes, or timers. Cloud providers dynamically manage the allocation of resources, automatically scaling the functions as needed to handle varying workloads. This approach abstracts away server management, enabling developers to focus on coding logic and functionality while benefiting from automatic scaling, reduced operational overhead, and pay-as-you-go pricing based on actual usage.Incorrect Answers:definitely not bare metal serversedge computing involves processing data closer to the source rather than a centralized cloudvirtual machines typically run long-running applicationshttps://glossary.cncf.io/serverless/

questions.question: What role requires experience with programming languages as well as operational tools such as deployment, monitoring, and logging?
options: Cloud Architect
Platform Engineer
DevOps Engineer
Application Developer
correct: DevOps Engineer
questions.overallExplanation: A DevOps engineer is responsible for bridging the gap between software development and IT operations. They focus on automating and streamlining the software delivery and infrastructure management processes. DevOps engineers work to ensure that applications are developed, tested, and deployed efficiently while maintaining system stability and security. Their role involves implementing continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines, managing infrastructure as code, and facilitating collaboration between development and operations teams to achieve faster and more reliable software delivery.https://www.cncf.io/blog/2022/07/01/devops-vs-sre-vs-platform-engineering-the-gaps-might-be-smaller-than-you-think/

questions.question: You are configuring authentication for your Kubernetes cluster and are considering various methods. Why is using a static password or token file generally not recommended for authenticating users in Kubernetes?
options: static files require frequent updates and maintenance
static files do not support multi-factor authentication (MFA)
static files increase the complexity of cluster configuration
static files are cleartext files that can be easily shared among users, compromising security
correct: static files are cleartext files that can be easily shared among users, compromising security
questions.overallExplanation: Using static password files or token files for authentication can pose a security risk because these files can be easily shared among users. This undermines the ability to ensure that only authorized individuals can access the cluster.Incorrect Answers:While static files do require updates when user access changes, this is not the primary reason why they are not recommended. The primary concern is the security risk associated with sharing static credentials.Static files may not necessarily increase the complexity of cluster configuration; in fact, they are relatively straightforward.While static files themselves may not directly support MFA, Kubernetes can integrate with authentication providers that support MFA, and MFA is a separate consideration from the use of static fileshttps://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/authentication/#users-in-kubernetes

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of using a CronJob resource in Kubernetes?
options: create persistent storage volumes for applications
facilitate inter-service communication in a microservices architecture
manage and automate recurring batch jobs or tasks
automatically scale pods based on CPU usage
correct: manage and automate recurring batch jobs or tasks
questions.overallExplanation: CronJobs in Kubernetes are designed to schedule and automate recurring batch jobs or tasks, allowing them to run at specified intervals or times.Incorrect Answers:CronJobs are not used for scaling pods based on CPU usage; this is typically handled by Horizontal Pod Autoscaling.Persistent storage volumes are managed using PersistentVolumeClaims and StorageClasses in Kubernetes, not CronJobs.CronJobs are not used for inter-service communication in a microservices architecture. They are focused on task automation rather than service-to-service communication.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/cron-jobs/

questions.question: What is a key reason for using Open Policy Agent (OPA) in a Kubernetes cluster?
options: monitor and log Kubernetes cluster events
enforce custom policies and governance on Kubernetes objects
directly manage Kubernetes resources and configurations
create container images for Kubernetes applications
correct: enforce custom policies and governance on Kubernetes objects
questions.overallExplanation: One of the primary use cases for OPA in Kubernetes is to enforce custom policies and governance rules on Kubernetes objects and resources. OPA allows you to define and evaluate policies to ensure your cluster meets specific security, compliance, and operational requirements.Incorrect Answers:OPA does not directly manage Kubernetes resources and configurations but evaluates policies against them.Creating container images for Kubernetes applications is a separate task and not the primary purpose of OPA.While OPA can assist with policy enforcement and auditing, its primary role is not to monitor and log cluster events.https://www.openpolicyagent.org/docs/latest/

questions.question: What component of Kubernetes is responsible for downloading the image and starting the container?
options: scheduler
container runtime
API server
kube-proxy
correct: container runtime
questions.overallExplanation: In Kubernetes, the container runtime is the component responsible for downloading container images and starting containers. Kubernetes relies on a container runtime, such as Docker or containerd, to perform these tasks. When you create a Pod in Kubernetes and specify which container images to use, the container runtime on each node in the cluster is responsible for fetching those images from container registries, ensuring their availability locally, and then launching the containers with the specified configurations. The container runtime handles the low-level operations needed to manage containers, while the Kubelet coordinates and manages these containers at the node level within the Kubernetes cluster.Incorrect Answers:the API server is the component of the Kubernetes control plane that exposes the Kubernetes APIthe scheduler watches for newly created Pods with no assigned node, and selects a node for them to run onkube-proxy is a network proxy that runs on each node in your clusterhttps://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/components/#container-runtime

questions.question: You're managing a Kubernetes cluster with different user groups, each with its own resource allocation and scheduling needs. To address this, you've started using Kubernetes Scheduler Profiles. What is the purpose of Scheduler Profiles?
options: define custom scheduling rules and configure the different stages of scheduling in the kube-scheduler
limit the total number of pods that can be scheduled in the cluster
automate the scaling of worker nodes based on resource demands
control the access and permissions of users within the cluster
correct: define custom scheduling rules and configure the different stages of scheduling in the kube-scheduler
questions.overallExplanation: Kubernetes Scheduler Profiles enable the definition of custom scheduling rules and resource allocations for different user groups or workloads. This allows you to tailor the scheduling behavior to specific needs, ensuring efficient resource utilization for diverse requirements.Incorrect Answers:Limiting the total number of pods is typically managed through other means, such as resource quotas.Access control and permissions are managed through Kubernetes RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) and are not directly related to Scheduler ProfilesScaling of worker nodes based on resource demands is typically managed by Kubernetes cluster autoscaling, which is separate from Scheduler Profileshttps://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/scheduling/config/#profiles

questions.question: You have a Kubernetes cluster with nodes that have specialized hardware accelerators (e.g., GPUs) and nodes without accelerators. You want to ensure that a specific set of pods, labeled as app=ml-app, always run on nodes with GPUs to maximize performance. Which Kubernetes feature should you use for this purpose?
options: Service Accounts
Resource Quotas
Taints and Tolerations
Node Affinity
correct: Node Affinity
questions.overallExplanation: In this scenario, you should use "Node Affinity" in Kubernetes. Node Affinity allows you to specify rules that influence pod placement based on node attributes. By defining node affinity rules with the appropriate label selectors, you can ensure that pods with the label "app=ml-app" are scheduled on nodes with GPUs, satisfying your hardware requirements and optimizing performance.Incorrect Answers:Taints and Tolerations are used to repel or tolerate pods on nodes but may not guarantee placement based on specialized hardware requirements.Resource Quotas are used to limit resource consumption (CPU, memory) within a namespace but do not dictate node selection.Service Accounts are used for managing access and permissions within pods but do not influence node placement based on hardware requirements.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity

questions.question: You are deploying a multi-tier application that includes a frontend and backend service. You want the frontend service to automatically find and connect to only healthy backend service instances. Which cloud-native networking solution would help meet this requirement?
options: service discovery
eBPF
service registration
pod network
correct: service discovery
questions.overallExplanation: Service discovery in a Kubernetes environment involves automatically finding and accessing services within the cluster using DNS names, making it easier to connect and communicate between different parts of a microservices-based application.https://glossary.cncf.io/service-discovery/

questions.question: What cloud-native architecture feature enables the ability for an application or service to adjust resources based on user demand automatically?
options: containerization
CI/CD
microservices
autoscaling
correct: autoscaling
questions.overallExplanation: Autoscaling involves the automatic adaptation of resources like servers, virtual machines, or application instances in response to the varying demand for a particular application or service. This dynamic process ensures that the application remains capable of accommodating sudden increases or decreases in user traffic without burdening the system with excessive resources during high-demand periods or wasting resources during low-demand periods.Incorrect Answers:Microservices are small, standalone software components collaborating to form larger applications.CI/CD is the rapid process of developing and delivering software through continuous integration and deployment practices.Containerization bundles applications and their requirements for consistent and portable deployment.https://glossary.cncf.io/auto-scaling/

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes how a Kubernetes manifest uses a declarative model?
options: Kubernetes manifests are used to execute imperative commands directly on the cluster nodes.
Kubernetes manifests provide a procedural script for automating infrastructure changes.
Kubernetes manifests define the desired state of resources and let Kubernetes reconcile the current state with the desired state.
Kubernetes manifests allow you to specify the exact sequence of steps for deploying and managing containers.
correct: Kubernetes manifests define the desired state of resources and let Kubernetes reconcile the current state with the desired state.
questions.overallExplanation: Kubernetes manifests are written in a declarative manner, meaning they describe the desired configuration or state of resources (such as pods, services, and deployments) without specifying the sequence of steps or procedures to achieve that state. Kubernetes itself is responsible for managing and reconciling the current state with the desired state to ensure resources are in the correct state as specified in the manifest.Incorrect Answers:declarative models don't specify an exact sequence of events - they define a desired state and let the platform (Kubernetes) reconcile the current state so it matches the desired statedeclarative models don't provide scripts to do somethingdeclarative manifests aren't imperative - imperative is actually the opposite of declarativehttps://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/manage-kubernetes-objects/declarative-config/

questions.question: What limitation is associated with using node selectors in Kubernetes for pod scheduling?
options: node selectors require manual intervention for every pod deployment
node selectors cannot be used to schedule pods on worker nodes
node selectors are unable to target specific nodes based on labels
node selectors are not suitable for handling complex node selection criteria
correct: node selectors are not suitable for handling complex node selection criteria
questions.overallExplanation: Node selectors are relatively simple and are not designed to handle complex node selection criteria. For more intricate scheduling requirements, Kubernetes provides other features like node affinity and anti-affinity rules, which offer greater flexibility in pod placement decisions.Incorrect Answers:Node selectors can target specific nodes based on node labels.Node selectors can be used to schedule pods on worker nodes or any nodes with specific labels.Node selectors do not necessarily require manual intervention for every pod deployment; they can be set in pod specifications.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#nodeselector

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of etcd in a Kubernetes cluster?
options: to manage network communication between pods
to store container images for pod deployments
to execute control plane operations
to maintain cluster configuration and state data
correct: to maintain cluster configuration and state data
questions.overallExplanation: In a Kubernetes cluster, the cluster store, primarily facilitated by etcd, serves as a distributed key-value data store. Its fundamental role is to store and manage crucial configuration and state information about the cluster. This includes data about nodes, pods, services, configurations, and other vital components. Etcd ensures that the cluster remains consistent and reliable by providing a source of truth for cluster-wide data. It allows all Kubernetes components to access and synchronize information, making it an essential backbone for maintaining the desired state and ensuring seamless operations in a Kubernetes cluster.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/components/#etcd

questions.question: In Kubernetes, what is the primary role of a "Service," and how does it achieve this role?
options: to manage configuration settings for pods and maintain their state.
to automate the scaling of pods based on resource utilization and demand
to distribute container images efficiently across the cluster by using load balancing
to expose a set of pods and provide network access to them by defining a stable endpoint
correct: to expose a set of pods and provide network access to them by defining a stable endpoint
questions.overallExplanation: The primary role of a "Service" in Kubernetes is to expose a set of pods and provide network access to them by defining a stable endpoint. It achieves this by using labels and selectors to group pods and ensure that network traffic is properly routed to them.Incorrect Answers:Distributing container images efficiently across the cluster by using load balancing is not the primary role of a Service. Load balancing typically involves Ingress controllers or other components, not Services.To automate the scaling of pods based on resource utilization and demand is a function of Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaling, which is separate from the primary role of a Service.To manage configuration settings for pods and maintain their state is not a role of a Service. Configuration settings and state management are typically handled by ConfigMaps, Secrets, and StatefulSets, among other resources in Kubernetes.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/

questions.question: Why might you use multiple custom schedulers alongside the default scheduler in Kubernetes?
options: to simplify resource management within the cluster
to accommodate different workload requirements and policies
improve the performance of containerized applications
reduce the number of nodes in the cluster
correct: to accommodate different workload requirements and policies
questions.overallExplanation: The correct answer is to accommodate different workload requirements and policies. By using multiple custom schedulers alongside the default scheduler, you can tailor the scheduling logic to meet specific needs, such as high-priority workloads, resource constraints, or specialized hardware requirements.Incorrect Answers:Improving the performance of containerized applications is not typically the primary goal of using multiple custom schedulers. Schedulers focus on workload placement and resource allocation.Using multiple custom schedulers may add complexity to resource management, especially if not done thoughtfully. It is often used to address workload diversity rather than simplifying resource management.The number of nodes in the cluster is not directly affected by the use of multiple schedulers. Schedulers determine where pods are placed within the available nodes based on their policies and requirements.https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/extend-kubernetes/configure-multiple-schedulers/

questions.question: When using a GitOps practice, what happens when a merge request is approved to a Git repository?
options: The changes are manually deployed by the architect or engineer
Any existing resources or applications are destroyed and replaced based on the new configurations
Changes are automatically applied to the target system or platform
The changes are queued for deployment after an architect approves them
correct: Changes are automatically applied to the target system or platform
questions.overallExplanation: When a merge request is approved in the GitOps practice and subsequently pushed to a Git repository, the continuous deployment pipeline is triggered. The approved changes are automatically applied to the target environment, ensuring that the actual state of the system is reconciled based on the desired state as specified in the Git repository. This approach enables a declarative way of managing infrastructure and applications, promoting consistency, version control, and automated deployment processes.Incorrect Answers:Changes are not queued for deployment. They are applied immediately to the target system/platformChanges will not be manually deployed, as GitOps intends to remove manual intervention and automate deployment and changesExisting resources are only destroyed if they are no longer needed and do not match the desired state. https://about.gitlab.com/topics/gitops/

questions.question: In Kubernetes, what are the two primary update strategies for managing changes to a deployment?
options: Immediate Update and Gradual Update
Incremental and Non-Incremental Updates
Rolling Update and Recreate Update
Blue-Green and Canary Deployments
correct: Rolling Update and Recreate Update
questions.overallExplanation: Rolling Update and Recreate Update. These are the two primary update strategies in Kubernetes. Rolling updates gradually replace old instances with new ones to minimize downtime, while recreate updates replace all instances simultaneously, resulting in a brief downtime.Incorrect Answers:Incremental and Non-Incremental Updates are not standard terms used in Kubernetes for update strategies. While rolling updates can be considered incremental, this terminology is not widely used.Blue-Green and Canary Deployments are different deployment strategies in Kubernetes and are not the same as update strategies. They focus on releasing new versions alongside existing ones for testing or routing traffic.D) Immediate Update and Gradual Update are not standard terms in Kubernetes for update strategies. Kubernetes primarily uses Rolling Update and Recreate Update as the standard strategies for managing updates.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#updating-a-deployment

questions.question: In Kubernetes, what is the primary purpose of a PVC?
options: to request access to a specific amount of persistent storage for a pod
to specify the number of replicas for a deployment
to configure network policies for communication between pods
to define the resource limits for a pod
correct: to request access to a specific amount of persistent storage for a pod
questions.overallExplanation: A Persistent Volume Claim (PVC) in Kubernetes is used to request access to a specific amount of persistent storage for a pod. PVCs allow you to declare your pod's storage requirements and enable dynamic provisioning or binding to appropriate storage resources.Incorrect Answers:PVCs are not used to define resource limits for pods. Resource limits for CPU and memory are typically specified in the pod's configuration, not in PVCs.Specifying the number of replicas for a deployment is unrelated to the role of PVCs. Replica counts are managed in deployment or replica set configurations.PVCs do not configure network policies for communication between pods. Network policies in Kubernetes are defined separately from storage-related configurations and govern network access and communication rules between pods.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes

questions.question: In cloud-native architecture, what approach allows you to define the desired outcome of the architecture without writing a step-by-step procedure to achieve a goal?
options: declarative approach
simplified approach
imperative approach
procedural approach
correct: declarative approach
questions.overallExplanation: In cloud-native architecture, the approach that allows you to define the desired outcome of the architecture without writing a step-by-step procedure to achieve a goal is the declarative approach. Declarative architecture focuses on specifying the desired end state or configuration, leaving it to the system or platform to determine how to achieve that state. This approach simplifies management, promotes automation, and abstracts the complexity of implementation from the user or developer, making it a fundamental principle in cloud-native design.https://www.cncf.io/blog/2022/02/17/increase-stability-and-reliability-with-kubernetes-helm-flux/

questions.question: Which Kubernetes controller is responsible for managing workloads that require persistent data?
options: Data
Deployment
StatefulSet
Scheduler
correct: StatefulSet
questions.overallExplanation: The type of Kubernetes controller responsible for managing workloads that require persistent data is the StatefulSet controller. StatefulSets are a specialized controller designed to handle stateful applications that need stable network identities and persistent storage.StatefulSets are used for applications like databases, messaging queues, and stateful microservices, where each pod has a unique identity and maintains its own state. They provide guarantees for pod ordering, stable network identities, and support for scaling operations with minimal disruption. StatefulSets are ideal for workloads that require persistent data because they ensure that pods are created and scaled in a predictable and orderly fashion, making it suitable for applications that rely on data consistency and stability.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/statefulset/

questions.question: What are the benefits of implementing a plugin architecture for various Kubernetes components, including CSI, CNI, and SMI interfaces?
options: to enforce vendor lock-in to ensure customers can use solutions from only supported vendors
to introduce overhead for communications between plugins and the targeted backend
eliminates security vulnerabilities within a Kubernetes environment
users can select the service or solution that suits their needs without vendor or technological lock-in concerns.
correct: users can select the service or solution that suits their needs without vendor or technological lock-in concerns.
questions.overallExplanation: Implementing a plugin architecture for various Kubernetes components, such as the Container Storage Interface (CSI), Container Networking Interface (CNI), and Service Mesh Interface (SMI), offers a range of advantages that enhance the flexibility and adaptability of the Kubernetes ecosystem. This approach promotes modularity, allowing different components to evolve independently without necessitating a complete system overhaul. It enables seamless integration of new features, improvements, and third-party solutions, facilitating customization to suit diverse cluster requirements.https://www.cncf.io/blog/2019/08/19/how-kubernetes-works/

questions.question: When measuring the performance of a system, SLO/SLA/SLI are commonly used to define targets or goals. What does the acronym SLI stand for?
options: Service Level Indicator
Server Latency Index
Software License Integration
System Log Inspector
correct: Service Level Indicator
questions.overallExplanation: SLI stands for "Service Level Indicator." It is a quantitative measurement used to evaluate the performance and availability of a service or system. SLIs help organizations set specific goals and monitor how well their services are meeting those goals. Common SLIs might include:Response Time: The time taken for a service to respond to a request.Latency: The delay between a request being made and the corresponding response being received.Error Rate: The percentage of requests that result in errors or failures.Throughput: The number of successful requests processed by the system over a given time period.Availability: The proportion of time that a service is operational and accessible.Uptime: The duration a system remains operational without experiencing downtime.https://www.cncf.io/blog/2020/11/13/a-guide-to-setting-up-kubernetes-service-level-objectives-slos-with-prometheus-and-linkerd/

questions.question: You have written a new Kubernetes manifest file named bk-app.yaml as shown below. What command would you use to deploy this file to your cluster?
options: kubectl get bk-app -o wide
kubectl describe bk-app.yaml
kubelet deploy bk-app.yaml
kubectl apply -f bk-app.yaml
correct: kubectl apply -f bk-app.yaml
questions.overallExplanation: Using kubectl apply is a fundamental command in Kubernetes for deploying a manifest. It allows you to declaratively specify the desired state of your resources by applying a configuration file, typically written in YAML or JSON format. When you execute kubectl apply, Kubernetes takes care of creating, updating, or deleting resources as needed to match the defined state in your manifest. This streamlined approach simplifies the deployment process, ensuring that your applications and services run consistently and reliably within the cluster.https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/cheatsheet/#kubectl-apply

questions.question: What component in a Kubernetes cluster is responsible for running workloads and applications?
options: control-plane
node
scheduler
kube-proxy
correct: node
questions.overallExplanation: In a Kubernetes cluster, the node type responsible for running workloads and applications is called a node (aka worker nodes). Nodes are responsible for hosting and executing the containers or pods that make up your applications. They run the workloads, monitor their health, and report back to the control plane. Worker nodes are where your application's code and data are actually run, and they provide the necessary resources, such as CPU, memory, and networking, for your applications to function.Incorrect Answers:control-plane nodes are the nodes where the control plane services are runapplication and container nodes are just made up terms for this questionhttps://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/architecture/nodes/

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of using 'Pod Anti-Affinity' in Kubernetes scheduling?
options: to prevent pods from being scheduled on the same node, promoting fault tolerance and reliability
to ensure that pods with a specific label are scheduled on the same node for enhanced performance
to prioritize pods with the same label for scheduling on the same nodes, improving load balancing
to guarantee that pods always run on the node with the most available resources
correct: to prevent pods from being scheduled on the same node, promoting fault tolerance and reliability
questions.overallExplanation: The primary purpose of using 'Pod Anti-Affinity' in Kubernetes scheduling is to enhance fault tolerance and reliability within the cluster. 'Pod Anti-Affinity' rules are employed to prevent multiple pods with the same anti-affinity label from being scheduled on the same node. By doing so, it ensures that if one node experiences issues or failures, the associated pods remain distributed across different nodes, reducing the risk of a single point of failure and enhancing the overall resilience of the application. This strategy is crucial for maintaining high availability and ensuring that the cluster can continue to operate smoothly, even in the presence of node failures or other disruptions.Incorrect Answers:scheduling pods on the same node with a specific label on the same node for performance reasons'Pod Anti-Affinity,' which focuses on avoiding co-location, not nodes with the most resourcesscheduling on the same nodes is pod affinity, the opposite of anti-affinityhttps://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of etcd in a Kubernetes cluster?
options: to execute control plane operations
to maintain cluster configuration and state data
to manage network communication between pods
to store container images for pod deployments
correct: to maintain cluster configuration and state data
questions.overallExplanation: In a Kubernetes cluster, the cluster store, primarily facilitated by etcd, serves as a distributed key-value data store. Its fundamental role is to store and manage crucial configuration and state information about the cluster. This includes data about nodes, pods, services, configurations, and other vital components. Etcd ensures that the cluster remains consistent and reliable by providing a source of truth for cluster-wide data. It allows all Kubernetes components to access and synchronize information, making it an essential backbone for maintaining the desired state and ensuring seamless operations in a Kubernetes cluster.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/components/#etcd

questions.question: Rather than running a container directly, what does Kubernetes use to schedule and run the container?
options: a Helm chart
a serverless function
a virtualized host
a Pod
correct: a Pod
questions.overallExplanation: A Pod is a group of one or more containers, with shared storage and network resources, and a specification for how to run the containers. A Pod's contents are always co-located, co-scheduled, and run in a shared context. A Pod models an application-specific "logical host": it contains one or more application containers that are relatively tightly coupled. In non-cloud contexts, applications executed on the same physical or virtual machine are analogous to cloud applications executed on the same logical host.Incorrect Answers:a Helm chart is a package format for defining, installing, and managing Kubernetes applicationsalthough a container might run on top of on a virtualized host, it's scheduled in a Podcontainers aren't run using serverless functionshttps://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/

questions.question: Which is NOT a benefit of using open standards when developing or integrating different technologies?
options: promotes interoperability, portability, and vendor neutrality
helps lock in users to a particular vendor or product
developed through a collaborative and consensus-based process
enables different technologies and products to work together seamlessly
correct: helps lock in users to a particular vendor or product
questions.overallExplanation: Open standards refer to specifications, formats, or protocols that are publicly accessible and collaboratively developed, fostering interoperability, portability, and vendor neutrality. This approach ensures seamless collaboration between various technologies and products, allowing them to function harmoniously. By adhering to open standards, cloud-native technologies work together cohesively, enabling developers to create applications across different services with confidence in their compatibility. This flexibility empowers users to switch between technologies without vendor lock-in, promoting competition and innovation. As vendors strive to offer superior products, the open standards framework catalyzes progress in the cloud-native ecosystem, benefiting developers, users, and the industry as a whole.Incorrect Answer:the point of using an open standard is so users will NOT get locked in to a specific vendor or cloud platform, giving them the flexibility to move to another solution easilyhttps://medium.com/devops-mojo/kubernetes-open-standards-oci-cri-cni-csi-smi-cpi-overview-what-is-k8s-open-standards-introduction-a860905af6f7

questions.question: Which organization is responsible for maintaining the Kubernetes project and serves as the governing body for any updates and changes?
options: Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF)
Google
HashiCorp
Linux Foundation
correct: Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF)
questions.overallExplanation: The organization responsible for maintaining the Kubernetes project and serving as the governing body for any updates and changes is the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF). Kubernetes was originally developed by engineers at Google, and in 2015, it was open-sourced and donated to the CNCF, which is a vendor-neutral organization focused on advancing cloud-native computing and promoting open-source projects related to container orchestration, microservices, and more. The CNCF oversees the development and evolution of Kubernetes, ensuring it remains an open and collaborative project with contributions from various companies and individuals in the community.https://www.cncf.io/projects/kubernetes/

questions.question: Workload-related objects, such as statefulsets and deployments, are defined in what popular API group?
options: core/v1
storage.k8s.io
apps/v1
workload/v1
correct: apps/v1
questions.overallExplanation: Workload-related objects like StatefulSets and Deployments are defined in the popular "apps" API group in Kubernetes. This API group is specifically designed for managing application deployments and stateful workloads, providing essential features for orchestrating containerized applications effectively.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#creating-a-deployment

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of using annotations in a Kubernetes definition file?
options: add metadata and information to resources for documentation, monitoring, or auditing purposes
specify resource limits for pods and containers
categorize and identify resources based on key-value pairs
configure network policies for pod communication
correct: add metadata and information to resources for documentation, monitoring, or auditing purposes
questions.overallExplanation: The primary purpose of using annotations in a Kubernetes definition file is to add metadata and information to resources for documentation, monitoring, or auditing purposes. Annotations are not used for core resource management but provide a way to attach additional data to resources, helping with documentation, tool integration, and other metadata-related requirements.Incorrect Answers:specifying resource limits for pods and containers is typically done using the resources field in the pod's configuration, not through annotationswhile labels are used for categorizing and identifying resources based on key-value pairs, annotations serve a different purpose by providing metadataconfiguring network policies for pod communication is typically done using Network Policies or other networking-related configurations, not annotationshttps://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/

questions.question: What is the main purpose of using namespaces in Kubernetes?
options: distribute workloads across multiple clusters for high availability and redundancy
provide secure access control and authorization at the cluster level, enhancing cluster security
segregate pods, services, and other resources into distinct virtual clusters within a single physical cluster
isolate nodes within a cluster, ensuring that they do not interfere with each other's resources
correct: segregate pods, services, and other resources into distinct virtual clusters within a single physical cluster
questions.overallExplanation: Kubernetes namespaces provide a way to create isolated virtual clusters within a single physical cluster. They enable resource organization and separation, making it simpler to manage and secure applications, especially in multi-tenant environments. Namespaces help maintain order and enforce access controls within the same Kubernetes cluster infrastructure.Incorrect Answers:Kubernetes namespaces are not used to isolate nodes; they are used for isolating and organizing resources within nodes.While access control is important, namespaces primarily focus on resource organization and isolation, not cluster-wide security.Kubernetes namespaces do not distribute workloads across multiple clusters; they are used to manage resources within a single cluster.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/namespaces/

questions.question: What does the term horizontal scaling refer to in the context of system architecture?
options: improving software code to make applications run faster and smoother
consolidating multiple virtual machines into a single, larger server to simplify management and reduce costs
increasing the capacity of a system by adding more machines or nodes
optimizing a single machine's performance to handle increased traffic efficiently
correct: increasing the capacity of a system by adding more machines or nodes
questions.overallExplanation: Horizontal scaling, often referred to as "scaling out," is a technique in system architecture where the capacity of a system is expanded by adding more machines or nodes. This approach aims to enhance performance and handle increased demands by distributing the workload across multiple instances. Unlike vertical scaling that involves upgrading a single machine, horizontal scaling focuses on the addition of resources, making it a preferred strategy for accommodating growth and ensuring efficient response to varying levels of traffic.https://glossary.cncf.io/horizontal-scaling/

questions.question: Which best represents the definition of Continuous Integration (CI)?
options: Automating the deployment of application updates from dev and test environments all the way up to production.
Automating the testing of the infrastructure used to run workloads in production environments.
Automating the deployment of application code to production environments, eliminating manual changes which may reduce system stability.
Automating the building and testing of application code with the goal of identifying issues before they reach the live environment.
correct: Automating the building and testing of application code with the goal of identifying issues before they reach the live environment.
questions.overallExplanation: Continuous Integration focuses on automating application builds and tests to ensure stable code. Commonly, am automated pipeline will be triggered when a merge request is approved, which will build the software and run associated tests against it. Code that passes tests in the CI stage gets passed to the CD stages for deployment.Incorrect Answers:CI is all about building and testing, not the deployment of application code (that's CD)CI is not responsible for promotion of code between dev and test environments to production. That's more of a definition of GitOps.CI does not test infrastructure that is used to run workloadshttps://glossary.cncf.io/continuous-integration/

questions.question: What are the benefits of using Git to store and manage both infrastructure and application code?
options: Git ensures that all team members are working on the same version of code, as it only supports a single repository and feature branch
Git ensures code is stored locally on team member's workstations and laptops
Git allows you to track changes, revert to previous versions, and collaborate effectively for infrastructure resources and applications
Git enables organizations to perform frequent manual changes to an environment
correct: Git allows you to track changes, revert to previous versions, and collaborate effectively for infrastructure resources and applications
questions.overallExplanation: Using Git to store and manage both infrastructure and application code offers numerous benefits that streamline development, deployment, and collaboration processes. Firstly, Git provides robust version control, allowing developers and operations teams to track changes, revert to previous versions, and maintain an organized history of modifications for both code and infrastructure configurations. This history acts as a single source of truth, ensuring consistency across environments and reducing the risk of configuration drift or inconsistencies between development, testing, and production setups.Incorrect Answers:Git doesn't enable manual changes, it allows for automated changes when paired with an orchestrator like ArgoCD or FluxWhile Git will store code on your team member's laptops and workstations, the benefit of Git would be a centralized repository, not distributed across a bunch of laptopsGit supports many repositories, code versions, and feature brancheshttps://www.atlassian.com/git/tutorials/gitops

questions.question: You have an application running on a Kubernetes cluster but want to manually scale out the number of pods. What is the process to scale the number of pods without impacting existing workloads in the deployment?
options: Add a load balancer server to the definition file and indicate the number of targets. Deploy the updated definition using the kubectl update deployment/app
Run the command kubectl deploy --scale=5
Create a new definition file that indicates the desired number of pods to add to the deployment. Apply the definition file using kubectl update -f bryan.yaml deployment/app
Modify the definition file and update the number of replicas to the desired number. Apply the new configuration using kubectl apply -f bryan.yaml
correct: Modify the definition file and update the number of replicas to the desired number. Apply the new configuration using kubectl apply -f bryan.yaml
questions.overallExplanation: To manually scale the number of pods for an application running on a Kubernetes cluster without impacting existing workloads, you can use the kubectl command-line tool to update the desired replica count of the corresponding deployment, replica set, or stateful set.https://jamesdefabia.github.io/docs/user-guide/kubectl/kubectl_apply/

questions.question: How can a single Kubernetes cluster be divided into virtual clusters, providing isolation and organization for workloads?
options: by partitioning the cluster into isolated namespaces
by physically separating nodes into distinct clusters
by creating separate clusters using different Kubernetes installations
by using network segmentation to isolate cluster resources
correct: by partitioning the cluster into isolated namespaces
questions.overallExplanation: Namespaces in Kubernetes can be used to partition the cluster into isolated namespaces. Each namespace acts as a logical boundary that allows you to organize and isolate resources (e.g., pods, services, config maps) within the same physical cluster. This provides a way to create virtual clusters within a single Kubernetes cluster for better resource management and access control.Incorrect Answers:Physically separating nodes into distinct clusters is not accomplished by using namespaces but by setting up separate Kubernetes clusters, each with its own set of nodes.Creating separate clusters using different Kubernetes installations is a valid approach to isolation but involves managing multiple Kubernetes installations separately. It is not the use of namespaces within a single cluster.Network segmentation can be used for network isolation, but it is not the primary purpose of Kubernetes namespaces. Namespaces primarily provide logical isolation for resources, while network policies are used for controlling network traffic between pods within namespaces.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/namespaces/

questions.question: What is a common name used for the kubectl configuration file?
options: kubecuttle
kubectl.yml
kubeconfig
kubeapp.json
correct: kubeconfig
questions.overallExplanation: The common name used for the kubectl configuration file is typically kubeconfig. This file stores information about the Kubernetes cluster, authentication credentials, and other configuration settings, allowing users to interact with and manage the cluster using the kubectl command-line tool. By default, kubectl looks for a file named "config" in the ~/.kube/ directory as the kubeconfig file, but you can specify a different file using the --kubeconfig flag when running kubectl commands.

questions.question: What typically happens when an image specified in a pod's configuration cannot be downloaded during deployment?
options: Kubernetes automatically substitutes the missing image with a default placeholder.
Kubernetes retries the image download periodically until it succeeds.
The deployment fails, and Kubernetes rolls back to the previous version.
The pod will display an error message like ErrImagePull or ImagePullBackOff.
correct: The pod will display an error message like ErrImagePull or ImagePullBackOff.
questions.overallExplanation: The pod remains in an ErrImagePull or ImagePullBackOff state until the image becomes available. Kubernetes will not proceed with the deployment of the pod until it can successfully download the specified container image. The pod will continue to be pending until the image is accessible.Incorrect Answers:Kubernetes does not automatically substitute missing images with default placeholders. It waits for the specified image to become available and does not make arbitrary substitutions.While a failed deployment may result in an error and potentially trigger a rollback (depending on the deployment strategy), this is not the typical behavior when an image cannot be downloaded. The deployment is paused until the image becomes available.Kubernetes does not retry image downloads indefinitely. It retries a certain number of times with a specified timeout, and if the image cannot be downloaded within those retries, the pod remains pending.https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/debug/debug-application/debug-pods/#my-pod-stays-pending

questions.question: Prometheus follows what type of model for collecting metrics from target systems?
options: Pull-Based Model
Push-Based Model
HTTP Request Model
Job-Based Query Model
correct: Pull-Based Model
questions.overallExplanation: Prometheus follows a "pull" model for collecting metrics from target systems. In this model, the Prometheus server actively fetches data from the target systems at regular intervals. The targets expose their metrics through HTTP endpoints, and Prometheus pulls this data from them. This approach gives Prometheus greater control over data collection and allows it to gather specific metrics according to its configuration.https://prometheus.io/docs/introduction/overview/#components

questions.question: Prometheus is an open-source monitoring tool designed to collect data from systems. From the list below, what type of data is Prometheus designed to collect?
options: events
logs
metrics
traces
correct: metrics
questions.overallExplanation: Prometheus is used to gather data about system performance, and it offers tools to help display this collected information. It is capable of sending alerts when the data reaches a level set by the user. This tool collects the data by fetching information from targets that make their metrics available using an HTTP endpoint. After collecting the data, it is stored in a time series database. This stored information can be searched and retrieved using a query language that comes built-in with Prometheus, the monitoring tool.Incorrect Answers:events are not collected by Prometheus, only metrics that contain numerical, time-based series datasystem logs are not collected by Prometheustraces are not collected by Prometheushttps://prometheus.io/

questions.question: What Kubernetes feature will ensure the scheduler distributes an application across user-defined fault zones, such as physical nodes?
options: load balancing
the API server
pod topology spread constraints
network policies
correct: pod topology spread constraints
questions.overallExplanation: To ensure that the scheduler distributes an application across user-defined fault zones, such as physical nodes, in Kubernetes, you should use Pod Topology Spread Constraints. This feature allows you to specify constraints on how pods should be distributed across different failure domains, such as nodes in different availability zones, regions, or any other custom-defined criteria. Pod Topology Spread Constraints ensure that your application pods are evenly distributed to enhance fault tolerance and reliability by preventing over-concentration in a single fault domain.https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/topology-spread-constraints/
